programs.name: Diamond State Health Plan
programs.what_it_does: Provides Medicaid coverage for healthcare services to eligible seniors. Includes personal care services and assistance with activities of daily living for those meeting medical requirements.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65 or over who are U.S. citizens, reside in Delaware, and meet medical and financial eligibility criteria.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income and medical needs; covers part or all of care costs including personal care services.[1]

programs.name: Division of Services for Aging and Adults with Physical Disabilities (DSAAPD) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services waivers to provide long-term care alternatives to nursing homes. Includes case management and support services through Area Agencies on Aging.[2][5]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ and adults with physical disabilities who are at risk of nursing home placement and meet income/asset limits.[2][5]
programs.benefit_value: Personal care, home modifications, and community services; value varies based on assessed needs.[2]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) - Delaware PACE
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits including adult day health care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible, able to live safely in the community, and enrolled in Medicare/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all approved services; no additional copays for participants.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB)
programs.what_it_does: QMB pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance. SLMB covers Medicare Part B premiums for eligible low-income seniors.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries residing in Delaware who are U.S. citizens meeting specific income and asset limits (varies by program).[1]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B cost-sharing; SLMB: Part B premium (amount varies by income).[1]

programs.name: Food Stamp Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits to purchase nutritious food. Administered statewide for low-income Delaware residents including seniors.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ meeting federal income and asset guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+ monthly per household; varies by income and household size.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Assists with heating and cooling bills and crisis fuel assistance. Helps prevent utility shutoffs for eligible households.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Delaware households including seniors aged 60+ based on income thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants averaging $400-$800 for heating assistance.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing. Reduces energy costs and improves health/safety for low-income homes.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Delaware households including seniors; priority for 60+ and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued at $5,000-$8,000 per household.

programs.name: Delaware Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP/SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare, supplements, and low-income assistance programs. Helps detect and prevent Medicare fraud through Area Agencies on Aging.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Delaware Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ and pre-retirees seeking insurance guidance.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling and educational presentations.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors. Provided through Area Agencies on Aging and nutrition programs.[2][4]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals, with priority for lowest income.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals per week; suggested contribution $2-3/meal or free for low-income.

programs.name: Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides support services including respite care, training, and counseling for family caregivers. Funded through the National Family Caregiver Support Program via AAAs.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of seniors aged 60+ or grandparents raising children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $500/year; training and support groups.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid work experience and training at community sites. Helps eligible seniors transition to unsubsidized employment.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors aged 55+ residing in Delaware.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/week) plus supportive services.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Offers free legal assistance for issues like housing, benefits, and consumer problems. Available through Area Agencies on Aging and legal services programs.[2][4]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities. Investigates complaints and ensures quality of care.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities in Delaware and their families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy and complaint resolution services.
